title: FISH in Ashbya
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"93vv5","text":"Abstract: This is Fluorescent in situ Hybridization for looking at transcripts in  Ashbya. If using this to look at RNA, then try to use all RNase-free material at the RNA bench. This was adapted from Therese Gerbich's protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5lkjb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"84g0s","text":"Keywords: Ashbya, Fluorescence, RNA,  DNA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Grow 100mL culture of Ashbya from dirty spores, seeded at2-3 µL spores/mL culture at 30 deg for 15 hours in 1000 ml baffled flask. Start around 4.30 PM.

Add 10ml of 37% formaldehyde (final 3.7%) and shake for 1hr at 30 deg. Alternatively, first, spin cells down in 15 mL tubes, resuspend in 9 mL used media and add 1mL formaldehyde, to decrease PFA waste.

Spin in 15 ml conicals (Important! Do NOT use 50 mL tubes) at 400 rpm in tabletop centrifuge for 5 mins, discard supernatant in formaldehyde waste. Aspirate using 5 mL pipette vertically to avoid disturbing pellet.
Wash 2x with 5 mL ice-cold Buffer B. Make sure you have a visible white pellet at the end of this step. During this time make fresh spheroplasting buffer
Resuspend in 1 ml spheroplasting buffer and transfer to a new 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube.

Add 100 𝞵l zymolyase (15 mg/ml) and incubate on a nutattor at 37 deg. Check digestion every 5- 10 mins until cells phase dark. (T his varies by day and by strain. Do not over-digest or cells will lyse)

Once phase dark, spin at 2000 rpm (digested cells are very fragile) for 2 mins in benchtop microfuge, wash 2x with 1 mL ice-cold buffer B. At this stage, the cells will become hard to pellet. Aspirate around suspended clumps. Setting the tube in a stand for 2 minutes before aspirating media helps decrease cell loss. Don’t worry about saving the hard-to-pellet cells, they are usually fragments of cells.
If doing proteinase k digestion
Add a solution of 2.5 𝞵l Proteinase K in 500 𝞵l wash buffer to pellet and tap to mix.
Let cells sit in the proteinase k solution for 5 minutes at room temperature. Don’t let reaction proceed beyond this or cells will lyse.
Spin at 2000 rpm for 2 mins and remove supernatant.

Add 500 𝞵l of 1mM PMSF in wash buffer to the cells, invert, and let it sit for 5 minutes. This needs to be made fresh every time as PMSF is very unstable in aqueous solutions. Check that PMSF hasn’t crashed out of solution in the stock.
Wash once with wash buffer. Add 1 ml 70% RNAse free EtOH leave overnight at 4 deg. Can be left in EtOH for a week.

Spin final time to remove Buffer B and resuspend in 1 ml 70% RNAse free EtOH leave overnight at 4 deg. Cells should be in RNAse free ethanol at 4 deg for at least 4 h. The cells may be stored in EtOH at 4 deg for up to a week.
Prepare hybridization solution: 100 𝞵l hybridization buffer with 1-3 𝞵l probe. Vortex to mix and centrifuge to remove any droplets.
Warm the hybridization solution to room temperature before opening

For initial tests of probes, it is best to start 4 separate hybridization reactions by adding 1 𝞵l each of the 1:10, 1:20, 1:50, 1:100 working dilutions to see which is optimal. CLN3 and BNI1 seem to work well using 1-2 𝞵lof 25 𝞵M probe
Centrifuge cells at 2000 rpm for 2 mins and remove ethanol.
Resuspend in 1 ml of wash buffer let stand at room temperature 2-5 mins
Centrifuge samples and remove wash buffer. Add hybridization solution and incubate in the dark overnight at 37 deg on a nutattor.
Add 1 ml wash buffer to the sample, invert to mix, centrifuge (2000 rpm for 2 mins), and remove supernatant.
Add 1 mL wash buffer and incubate at 37 deg for 30 min (to save time can add Hoechst to wash buffer)
If not performing GFP booster
Spin down cells, remove supernatant, resuspend in 500 𝞵l wash buffer (can be skipped).
Add 1𝞵l Hoescht and incubate in the dark at room temperature for 15 min. (unless done previously in step 15)

Wash with 500 𝞵l wash buffer. Remove as much supernatant as possible . At this point , you may not even see a pellet. That’s okay, as long as you had a small pellet till the previous step, you’ll see cells later.

Add enough mounting medium to roughly equal the volume of cells. Use a minimum of 20 𝞵l. Can increase if there are a lot of cells. Scrape the walls to get out the last of the cells, but make sure you don’t make bubbles. Pipette up and down slowly to mix.

Mount 20 𝞵l on coverslip then place the slide on top. For coverslip use 22x50mm. ( This is important to not lose cells ). Gently and flatten with a notebook with something heavy on the slide to help flatten Ashbya.
After about 10-15 mins of flattening, seal with nail polish. Slides may be stored at -20 deg.
If performing GFP booster
Wash 2x with 250ul 1x-PBS + 1 mg/ml BSA. Resuspend a final time in 250 𝞵l PBS + BSA incubate in the dark at room temperature for 30 min to block.
Wash 2x with 250 𝞵l PBS+BSA
Resuspend in 200 𝞵l PBS +BSA and add 1 l𝞵 GFP booster and incubate overnight in the dark at 4 deg
On the next day, wash 2X with 250 𝞵l PBS+BSA
Resuspend in 500 𝞵l PBS+BSA and 1ul Hoechst and incubate in the dark at room temperature for 30 min.
Wash with 500ul PBS + BSA, remove as much supernatant as possible.
Add enough mounting media to roughly equal the volume of the cells. Use a minimum of 20 𝞵l.

Mount 20 𝞵l on coverslip then place the slide on top. For coverslip use 22x50mm. ( This is important to not lose cells ). Gently and flatten with a notebook with something heavy on the slide to help flatten Ashbya.
After about 10-15 mins of flattening, seal with nail polish. Slides may be stored at -20 deg.
creator: Sierra Cole
